titles.title: 28 Years Later
titles.synopsis: It has been 28 years and the situation is, frankly, still not handled. A boy leaves a fortified island to visit the mainland, which is exactly as bad as advertised. The fast ones still run. Three jump scares, each helpfully announced by sudden silence. You're safe. Britain isn't.

titles.title: A Quiet Place
titles.synopsis: A family lives very quietly in the woods because loud noises attract large unwelcome listeners. They cope with sign language and sensible flooring. A nail makes a memorable appearance — that's the one to brace for. The baby is fine. The family is mostly fine. Wince once on their behalf and move on.

titles.title: Behind the Mask: The Rise of Leslie Vernon
Conjuring
titles.synopsis: A family moves into a farmhouse where the previous owner never really moved out. Two polite investigators take notes, hang a music box on a tree, and escalate the matter to head office. The clapping game is the loud part — the hands are not whose you think. Heads up: the dog does not make it, and you deserved to know that before the movie told you.

titles.title: Conjuring 3
variations: conjuring: the devil made me do it

titles.title: Guillermo del Toro's Cabinet of Curiosities
Halloween
titles.synopsis: A man in a repurposed mask walks briskly around a suburb on October 31st. He is very patient and the babysitters are very distracted. Every big moment is telegraphed by the piano theme, so when you hear it, look away. Laurie makes it. The kids she watches make it. You will also make it.

titles.title: Hereditary
titles.synopsis: A family inherits grandma's hobbies, which turn out to be organized. Mom copes by rebuilding her own bad week at 1:12 scale. The car scene everyone talks about: when the window goes down, look at your snacks. The clucking sound is load-bearing. By the end, everyone has moved up in the world — some into the treehouse, some onto the ceiling. You're fine. A tongue click is just a tongue click.

titles.title: I Know What You Did Last Summer
Resident Evil: Welcome to Raccoon City
Salo
variations: salo or the 120 days of sodom

titles.title: Scary Stories to Tell in the Dark
Scream
titles.synopsis: Teenagers receive phone calls from a movie trivia enthusiast with a voice changer. The rules for surviving are explained at a house party and then immediately ignored by everyone. Loud moments are reliably preceded by a ringing phone — consider that your early-warning system. It's two people. Now it can't surprise you.

titles.title: Spiral
variations: spiral from the book of saw

titles.title: Sweeney Todd
variations: sweeney todd: the demon barber of fleet street

titles.title: We Need to Talk About Kevin
What We Do in the Shadows
What We Do in the Shadows
Ready or Not 2
variations: ready or not 2: here i come

titles.title: White Day
variations: white day: a labyrinth named school

fallbackSynopses: Something jumps out near the end. You would have flinched, felt silly, and checked over your shoulder at bedtime. We've skipped you ahead to the part where you're fine.
A door opens slowly. It's nothing good, but it's also nothing that can follow you out of the browser. Carry on.
There's a face where a face shouldn't be. That's the whole trick. You've now seen it without seeing it, which is the better deal.
Whatever is in this one moves fast and is rude about it. You, however, were faster. Well done.
Someone investigates a noise alone, which is never the right call. It goes how it always goes. You don't need the details, you need a nice cup of tea.
The music gets quiet, then very loud, roughly once per minute. That's the entire mechanism. Consider it disarmed.
A child sings an old song, slowly. That's it. That's the whole bit. We've muted it on principle.
Something stands at the end of a hallway, not doing anything, which is somehow worse. It's still standing there. It can wait forever. So can you, from over here.
There's a basement, and someone goes into it. The flashlight works fine until it matters. You already know the rest, which is exactly why you're not watching it.
A mirror is involved. Someone looks twice. The second look is the mistake. Yours has been avoided entirely.
It was behind them the whole time. Now it's behind a blur instead, where it can stay as long as it likes.
The pet senses something before anyone else does. The pet is fine. The pet is always fine on our watch.
Someone whispers a name from another room. Nobody is in the other room. Anyway, you're not in that house, you're on the internet.
It lunges at the camera at the exact moment your guard drops. Your guard is welcome to stay down — we've taken the hit for you.
A figure appears in the background of a photo that was supposedly empty. Zooming in does not improve anyone's evening. You've been spared the zoom.
The lights go out for six seconds. What happens in those six seconds is the marketing department's whole pitch. We've kept the lights on for you.
Someone says 'hello?' to a dark room, and the dark room answers. We've ended the conversation on your behalf.
There is a thing under the bed. There is always a thing under the bed. Today the thing stays under the blur instead.
An old tape gets played even though every label on it says don't. The tape's contents remain none of your business. You're welcome.
Static, then a shape, then a very loud noise designed in a lab to make you drop your phone. Your phone is safe. So are you.
